zh.lifeSummary: Richard Allarde是Henry Allarde和Margaret Allard的兒子，於大約1533年出生在英國肯特比登登。他於1562年11月30日與Ellen Hammone在英國肯特可藍別盧可結婚。他們至少是3個兒子和4個女兒的父母。他去世於1593年6月3日在英國英格蘭肯特比登登享年61歲，埋葬在英國英格蘭肯特奧爾平頓哥吉拉 沙智 亞德。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: Richard Allarde是Henry Allarde和Margaret Allard的儿子，于大约1533年出生在英国肯特比登登。他于1562年11月30日与Ellen Hammone在英国肯特可藍別盧可结婚。他们至少是3个儿子和4个女儿的父母。他去世于1593年6月3日在英国英格兰肯特比登登享年61岁，埋葬在英国英格兰肯特奧爾平頓哥吉拉 沙智 亞德。

nameMeanings.name: Allarde
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Gilles, Andre, Emile, Normand, Marcel, Alain, Armand, Gaston, Pierre, Antoine, Benoit, Fernand.

English: from a Middle English personal name, Alard, Adelard, Aylard, or Elard. The former two names are usually of native English origin, from Old English Æthelheard, composed of the elements æthel ‘noble’ + heard ‘hard, bold’. Alternatively, the surname may be of continental origin, from an ancient Germanic name (see 2 below), which developed in Old French to Alard, and in Middle Dutch to Adelaert and Alaert. The continental names were used in post-Conquest England by Normans and Flemings. Later development of -ard to -ett and -att in surnames means that these personal names may also lie behind some examples of e.g. Allatt and Hallett .
French: from the medieval personal name Alard, from ancient Germanic Adelhard, composed of the elements adal ‘noble’ + hard ‘hardy’. Compare Allar , Allor , Allord , and Allore .

variants.name: Ellerd
meanings: English (Bedfordshire and Northamptonshire): variant of   Allard  . This form of the surname is now rare in Britain.
Probably an altered form of German Ellert, a variant of Ehlert.

variants.name: Ellert
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Merwin, Traude.

variants.name: Hallett
meanings: English (mainly Somerset and Dorset): variant of Allatt, itself a variant of Allard , with prosthetic H-.

nameMeanings.name: Richard
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: One of the most enduringly successful of the Old French personal names introduced into Britain by the Normans. It is of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from rīc ‘power’ + hard ‘strong, hardy’. It has enjoyed continuous popularity in England from the Conquest to the present day, influenced by the fact that it was borne by three kings of England, in particular Richard I ( 1157–99 ). He was king for only ten years ( 1189–99 ), most of which he spent in warfare abroad, taking part in the Third Crusade and costing the people of England considerable sums in taxes. Nevertheless, he achieved the status of a folk hero, and was never in England long enough to disappoint popular faith in his goodness and justice. He was also Duke of Aquitaine and Normandy and Count of Anjou, fiefs which he held at a time of maximum English expansion in France. His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

It has enjoyed continuous popularity in England from the Conquest to the present day, influenced by the fact that it was borne by three kings of England, in particular Richard I ( 1157–99 ). He was king for only ten years ( 1189–99 ), most of which he spent in warfare abroad, taking part in the Third Crusade and costing the people of England considerable sums in taxes. His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

historicEvents.title: Book of Common Prayer
historicEvents.description: Book of Common Prayer, a product of the English Reformation, was published in 1549 for assistance in the administration of the sacraments and other rites and ceremonies of the church according to the use of the Church of England. The book outlined morning, evening, and communion prayers and orders for baptism and marriage, thus making England a truly Protestant state.

historicEvents.title: Act of Uniformity
historicEvents.description: The Act of Uniformity was passed by the Parliament of England and required all people to go to church once a week. The consequence of not attending church was a fine of 12 pence, which was a considerable amount for a poor person.

historicEvents.title: State Lottery
historicEvents.description: A State Lottery was recorded in 1569. The tickets were sold at St. Paul's Cathedral in London.

historicEvents.title: The Royal Exchange is Organized
historicEvents.description: The Royal Exchange was organized in January 1571 by Sir Thomas Gresham. Gresham is known as the father of English banking.

historicEvents.title: The First English Colony is Established in America
historicEvents.description: England established its first colony in North America in 1585 as a way to generate additional wealth. The colony was named Virginia, after the virgin Queen Elizabeth I.

historicEvents.title: Elizabeth I Signs Death Warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots
historicEvents.description: Elizabeth I signed the death warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots, on February 1, 1587, as she was found guilty of plotting to assasinate Elizabeth. Mary, Queen of Scots, was beheaded at Fotheringhay Castle in England on February 8, 1587.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Elizabeth I.org - Mary Queen of Scots

en.lifeSummary: Richard Allarde was born about 1533, in Biddenden, Kent, England as the son of Henry Allarde and Margaret Allard. He married Ellen Hammone on 30 November 1562, in Cranbrook, Kent, England. They were the parents of at least 3 sons and 4 daughters. He died on 3 June 1593, in Biddenden, Kent, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 61, and was buried in All Saints Churchyard, Orpington, Kent, England, United Kingdom.

Il a épousé Ellen Hammone le 30 novembre 1562, à Cranbrook, Kent, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 3 fils et 4 filles. Il est décédé le 3 juin 1593, à Biddenden, Kent, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 61 ans, et a été enterré à All Saints Churchyard, Orpington, Kent, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni.

ja.lifeSummary: Richard Allardeは約1533年にイギリスケントビッドエンデンでHenry AllardeとMargaret Allardの息子として生まれた。1562年11月30日にイギリスケントクランブルークでEllen Hammoneと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも3人の息子と4人の娘の親であった。彼は1593年6月3日にイギリスイギリスケントビッドエンデンで61歳で死去し，イギリスイギリスケントオルパントンアル サインツ チャーチヤルドにて埋葬された。

ko.All Saints Churchyard, Biddenden, Ashfor: 영국 잉글랜드 켄트 르핀그턴 앨 세인츠 c후르c히아어드
ko.lifeSummary: Richard Allarde(은)는 대략 1533년 영국 켄트 비데든에서 Henry Allarde(와)과 Margaret Allard의 아들로 태어났다. 그는 1562년 11월 30일 영국 켄트 크랜브룩에서 Ellen Hammone(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 세 명의 아들과 네 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1593년 6월 3일 영국 잉글랜드 켄트 비데든에서 61세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 잉글랜드 켄트 르핀그턴 앨 세인츠 c후르c히아어드에 묻혔다.

ru.lifeSummary: Richard Allarde родился около 1533 г. в местности бидденден, кэнт, Англия. Его родителей звали Henry Allarde и Margaret Allard. Он женился на женщине по имени Ellen Hammone 30 ноября 1562 г. в местности кранбрук, кэнт, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 3 сына и 4 дочери. Он умер 3 июня 1593 г. в местности бидденден, кэнт, Англия, Великобритания в возрасте 61 год и был похоронен в местности алл сеинц чурчярд, орпингтон, кэнт, Англия, Великобритания.
